1.hint: Time passed equals end minus start — 2 minutes from 0.
1.help:
**2 minutes** have passed.

Elapsed time on horizontal axis.

Answer 2 minutes.

2.help:
1 + 1 = **2 metres** total.

Distance adds along journey.

2 m travelled.

3.help:
At the **start**, distance from home is **0** (if graph starts at origin).

Beginning point.

0 metres at start.

4.hint: Flat distance means no change in position.
4.help:
Distance not changing → **not moving** (stationary).

Horizontal section.

Object still.

5.hint: Subtract start height from end height on graph.
5.help:
3 − 0 = **3 metres** increase.

Rise on graph.

Increase 3 m.

6.help:
Graph shows **2 metres** at 1 minute.

Read vertical at given time.

2 metres.

7.hint: Read horizontal axis at the point showing 5 m.
7.help:
Time is **3 minutes**.

Coordinates $(3,5)$.

3 minutes.

Write each step in order so you can use the same method on a similar question later.

8.hint: Line going up means distance growing.
8.help:
Distance is **increasing**.

Upward slope.

Increasing.

Write each step in order so you can use the same method on a similar question later.

9.hint: Flat section on distance-time = no distance change.
9.help:
**Stationary** — resting, not moving.

Zero speed section.

Person stopped.

10.hint: Steeper line = more distance per hour.
10.help:
**Faster** speed — greater gradient.

Steep = quick; gentle = slow.

Steeper means faster.

11.help:
**8 km** in 2 hours at 4 km/h.

Multiply rate by time.

8 km.

12.help:
$60 \div 2 = **30** km/h.

Average speed formula.

30 km/h.

Write each step in order so you can use the same method on a similar question later.

13.hint: Flat depth means fill rate paused or stopped.
13.help:
Water **not rising** — tap off or full level reached.

No depth change.

Not filling (or stable).

14.hint: Same flow into narrower space rises faster.
14.help:
**Steeper** graph — depth increases quicker in narrow container.

Same rate, less base area.

Steeper line.

15.help:
$20 \div 5 = **4** cm per minute.

Rate = rise ÷ time.

4 cm/min.

16.hint: Speeding up means changing gradient — curve or steeper segments.
16.help:
Speed **increasing** → graph **curves** upward (not one straight line).

Acceleration changes slope.

Curved or changing slope.

17.hint: Downward slope means distance from start decreasing.
17.help:
Moving **back toward start** or decreasing measured distance.

Negative gradient.

Returning / distance falling.

18.hint: Three phases → three sections on graph.
18.help:
**Three** sections: walk, flat rest, walk again.

Each stage its own slope.

Three parts.

19.help:
**15 km/h**.

Gradient of line.

15 km/h.

Write each step in order so you can use the same method on a similar question later.

20.help:
$3 \times 4 = **12** litres.

Rate times time.

12 litres.

Write each step in order so you can use the same method on a similar question later.

21.hint: Flat graph → zero gradient → zero speed.
21.help:
**Speed is zero** — not moving.

Horizontal means stationary.

No movement.

22.help:
**Higher speed** — more distance per unit time.

Gradient meaning.

Faster.

23.hint: Flat means zero speed, not slow walking.
23.help:
Priya wrong — **stopped**, not slow.

Gradient 0.

Stationary, not slow walk.

24.help:
**Always** true for distance-time graphs.

Gradient is speed.

Always.

25.hint: Bottle shape changes width — depth rise rate varies.
25.help:
Graph **curves** — steep/narrow sections and shallow/wide sections.

Not one straight line.

Sam wrong.

26.hint: Same time 2 h — compare distances 10 vs 20 km.
26.help:
**Second graph faster** — 20 km in 2 h beats 10 km.

Steeper to $(2,20)$.

Faster journey.

27.hint: Can you move closer to start while distance measured from start stays defined?
27.help:
From fixed start, distance usually **non-decreasing** on outbound journeys — negative gradient means returning.

Context dependent — statement often **true** for distance from start on simple journeys.

Discuss returning home.

28.hint: Steep depth rise = narrow container, not wide.
28.help:
Leo backwards — **steep** means **narrow** (depth jumps quickly).

Wide middle = shallow slope.

Correct interpretation.

29.hint: Still = flat line; steady walk = straight sloped line.
29.help:
Standing: **horizontal**. Constant speed walk: **straight slope**.

Different gradients.

Flat vs sloped.

30.hint: Cone filling changes cross-section area with height.
30.help:
Depth vs time **curves** — area grows with height in cone point-up.

Mo wrong.

Curved graph.

31.hint: Three legs: out steady, flat stop, back faster steady.
31.help:
Sloped up, **flat** 10 min, **steeper** slope back down/home.

Three sections described.

Out, rest, fast return.

32.help:
Upward slope while filling, **downward** while draining.

Two stages.

Up then down.

33.help:
$120 + 60 = **180** km total.

Two legs at different speeds.

180 km.

34.hint: Wide middle → slower depth rise; narrow ends → steep.
34.help:
Steep–shallow–steep pattern matching vase profile.

Same flow rate, varying width.

Explain three phases.

35.hint: Sprint rise, plateau high, rest drop.
35.help:
Quick **up**, flat **high**, **down** on rest.

Heart rate sections.

Three described parts.

36.hint: Gradient $90/3=30$ km/h; curve would mean changing speed.
36.help:
Average speed **30 km/h**.

Curve ⇒ speed not constant.

Straight = steady speed.

37.hint: 1 h at 20 km/h plus 1 h at 10 km/h.
37.help:
$20 + 10 = **30** km total.

Two hour segments.

30 km.

Write each step in order so you can use the same method on a similar question later.

38.hint: Cylinder uniform width → straight depth line; cone varies.
38.help:
**Cylinder** straight; **cone** curved.

Constant vs changing cross-section.

Compare shapes.

39.hint: Speed-time rising then flat → distance-time curves then straight slope.
39.help:
Accelerating phase: distance-time **curves**; constant speed: **straight** slope.

Link graphs.

Describe both stages.

40.hint: Gradient = rate — distance per time vs depth per time.
40.help:
Both use **steepness as rate** — km/h vs cm/min.

Same maths, different labels.

Explain each context.

41.hint: 2 km in 30 min = 2 km in 0.5 h.
41.help:
$2 \div 0.5 = **4** km/h.

Convert minutes to hours.

4 km/h.

42.help:
Graph slopes **down**.

Volume falling.

Downward slope.

Write each step in order so you can use the same method on a similar question later.
